JUNSU KIM Hi, I am a prospective Ph.D. student. I am looking for an opportunity to conduct research on my academical interests. Thanks.
  • home

7 AUG 2020 • interest

RESEARCH INTEREST

I am interested in building an intelligent memory, storage system via Machine Learning. Through my research, I hope that future computer system will overcome the fundamental, structural problems.

  • If you feel interested, Here is my CV

6 AUG 2020 • education

EDUCATION

Bachelor of Electronic Engineering [Mar 2014 - Feb 2021]

  • Hanyang University, Seoul (Korea, Republic of)

  • Overall GPA : 3.79/4.00 (Upperdivison GPA : 3.91/4.00)
  • Hanyang University Scholarship - Full Scholarship (7 Semesters)

5 AUG 2020 • projects

Detecting Rowhammer bitflips using ML

  • Problem : Revisiting RowHammer(ISCA’2020) showed only 10K activations can cause RowHammer bitflips and indicated that rowhammer threshold decreases if the device feature size gets smaller

  • Observation : Related works(Deterministic, Probabilistic approach)’ area and energy overhead increased or their performance became lower when the rowhammer threshold decreased

  • Idea: Detecting rowhammer bitflips using ML

  • Implementation : Still working on this (since 2020-09-01)

31 JUL 2020 • projects

Implementation of Retention time Aware Intelligent DRAM Refresh in DDR4

  • Problem : Refresh operations interfere memory accesses and waste energy

  • Observation : Energy and performance overhead caused by refresh operations rised when the device integrity increased

  • Idea: Applied different refresh rate for strong DRAM cells (idea from RAIDR(ISCA’2012))

  • Implementation : Bloomfilter to store the information of weak rows in memory controller, DDR4-2133 configuration file

  • Evaluation : Used DRAMSim2 and gem5 as simulators, memory intensive benchmarks from SPEC CPU2006

  • Contribution : Came up with the result that RAIDR averaged 12% performance improvement over auto-refresh scheme in DDR4

19 JUN 2020 • projects

Accelerating Deeplearning Workloads using Compiler

  • Conducted in Cass Lab in Hanyang University, Advised by Prof. Yongjun Park

  • Problem : Finding optimal compuation graphs for each architecture requires much of experts’ hand-made effort

  • Observation : Related work (TASO(SOSP’2019)) was not able to generate optimal computation graphs for CPU

  • Idea : Build a system that automatically generates optimal computation graphs for inference on CPU

20 JUN 2019 • projects

CAPTCHA PROJECT : Building a Machine Reads Distorted Text

  • Built a system that can read distorted text

  • Problem : A small train set(only 1000 samples) caused overfitting and a low accuracy

  • Idea : Increased the amount of the training set to avoid overfitting

  • Implementation : Used data augmentation approach and Transfer Learning

  • Contribution : Achieved the highest score at the class kaggle competition with only 1000 training samples (dataset from Wilhelmy, Rodrigo Rosas, Horacio)

  • Result :

20 MAY 2019 • projects

Implementing HDL Programs in FPGA

  • Got used to FPGA platform and Verilog by coding FIR Filter, Mealy and Moore machine

  • Implemented LED, seven segment, VGA controller in FPGA

19 MAY 2019 • projects

Embedded System Software Design (Used Achro 210T Board)

  • Cross compiled bootloader and kernel & implemented them in the device

  • Coded the LED controller in Achro 210T board

  • Got used to ARM Assembly language
